message:
fix(scope): treat all-empty project scope identifiers as no-op

When apply_project_scope() receives a list of identifiers that are all
empty strings or whitespace-only (e.g. ["", "   "]), the previous code
would skip all identifier matching, compute an empty matched_ids set,
then derive disallowed = all project IDs, and proceed to delete every
project from the scoped view. This is clearly wrong -- if no valid
identifiers were provided, the caller's intent is "no filtering", not
"remove everything".

Add an early-return guard: after filtering out empty/whitespace
identifiers, if matched_ids is empty, return immediately with
removed_count=0 and the full project list intact.

Add test scope_only_empty_identifiers_keeps_all to verify the guard
preserves all projects and messages when only empty identifiers are
provided.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
